case_title: DESTINY EKWUEME VS. AARON BUHRZ ET AL

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 18093026), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF EKWUEME, DESTINY SERVED MAR-12-2018, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT BIG HAIR LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AARON BUHRZ AND BIG HAIR, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DESTINY EKWUEME IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FILING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND LATER IN THE CASE IF MS. EKWUEME IS ABLE TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SHOW THAT MR. BUHRZ AND/OR BIG HAIR IS LIABLE AS AN EMPLOYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER CIVIL CODE 3294(B). WHILE THE FAILURE OF NIGHT CLUB SECURITY PERSONNEL TO INTERVENE TO PROTECT THE SAFETY OF A PATRON WHERE THE PERSONNEL KNOW THAT THEIR FAILURE WILL RESULT IN SEVERE HARM TO THE PATRON COULD SUFFICE TO SHOW MALICE OR OPPRESSION FOR PURPOSES OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY, MS. EKWEUME DOES NOT ALLEGE, NOR DOES IT APPEAR THAT SHE IS NOW ABLE TO ALLEGE, THAT EITHER MR. BUHRZ OR A MANAGING AGENT OF BIG HAIR HAD ADVANCE KNOWLEDGE OF THE ALLEGED UNFITNESS OF THE SECURITY PERSONNEL AND EMPLOYED THEM WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE SAFETY OF OTHERS OR AUTHORIZED OR RATIFIED THE SECURITY PERSONNEL'S DECISION NOT TO INTERVENE TO PROTECT MS. EKWEUME. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-24-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Continued To Aug-15-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Aaron Buhrz and Big Hair, LLC's motion to strike the request for punitive damages in the complaint filed by plaintiff Destiny Ekwueme is granted without leave to amend but without prejudice to filing a motion for leave to amend later in the case if Ms. Ekwueme is able to allege in good faith sufficient facts to show that Mr. Buhrz and/or Big Hair is liable as an employer for punitive damages per Civil Code 3294(b). While the failure of night club security personnel to intervene to protect the safety of a patron where the personnel know that their failure will result in severe harm to the patron could suffice to show malice or oppression for purposes of punitive damages liability, Ms. Ekweume does not allege, nor does it appear that she is now able to allege, that either Mr. Buhrz or a managing agent of Big Hair had advance knowledge of the alleged unfitness of the security personnel and employed them with a conscious disregard for the safety of others or authorized or ratified the security personnel's decision not to intervene to protect Ms. Ekweume. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
